message:
fix(fcp-store): cap source_symbols pre-allocation to RFC 6330 K_MAX (br-ywpup)

Closes flywheel_connectors-ywpup (P1/HIGH).

Reviewer (cod_3 P4) flagged MemorySymbolStore::put_object_meta:

    let capacity = meta.source_symbols as usize;
    symbols: HashMap::with_capacity(capacity),

There was no validation on meta.source_symbols before the allocation.
Metadata for symbol repair comes from untrusted mesh/state inputs, so a
forged ObjectSymbolMeta with source_symbols = u32::MAX (or any very
large value) would force a multi-GB HashMap allocation under the
global `objects.write()` lock BEFORE any symbols arrive, exhausting
memory and stalling every concurrent symbol-store operation.

Fix (defense-in-depth):

1. Upfront rejection before taking objects.write():
   - Introduce MAX_SOURCE_SYMBOLS = 56_403 — the RFC 6330 §5.1.2 K_MAX
     that fcp_raptorq::encode::Encoder::new already enforces at the
     encode path.
   - Reject source_symbols == 0 or > K_MAX with
     SymbolStoreError::InvalidSymbol before any allocation or lock
     acquisition.
2. Bounded preallocation belt-and-braces:
   - capacity = min(source_symbols as usize, MAX_SOURCE_SYMBOLS as usize)
   - Future changes to the upfront check can't silently regress the
     allocation budget.

Added regression test `put_object_meta_rejects_oversized_source_symbols`
covering three attack shapes:
- K_MAX + 1 (straight above-bound)
- u32::MAX (the reviewer's worst-case attacker payload)
- 0 (zombie-entry case)
All three must yield InvalidSymbol, not allocation or panic.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-store --lib put_object_meta — 3/3 pass.
Full -p fcp-store: 802 pass, 2 pre-existing flaky failures
(offline::access_pattern_tracker_prioritized_objects +
quarantine::evict_expired) confirmed baseline-equivalent via stash-and-
rerun. +1 new passing test attributable to this fix; zero regression.

Cod_4 / SunnyMoose
